chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَجُوزُ مِنْ شَهَادَةِ النِّسَاءِ وَ مَا لَا يَجُوزُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – What is allowed from the testimony of the women, and what is not allowed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/240)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/240)
text: قَالَ لَا تَجُوزُ شَهَادَةُ النِّسَاءِ فِي الْهِلَالِ وَ لَا فِي الطَّلَاقِ
وَ قَالَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ النِّسَاءِ تَجُوزُ شَهَادَتُهُنَّ قَالَ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ فِي الْعُذْرَةِ وَ النُّفَسَاءِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from Abu Ayoub Al Khazzaz, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,
‘He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The testimony of the women is not allowed regarding the (sighting of the) new moon, nor regarding the divorce’.
And he (the narrator) said, ‘And I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the women, is their testimony allowed?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, regarding (establishing) the virginity, and the childbirth’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus from abu Ayyub al-Khazzaz from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has narrated the following: “He (the Imam) has said that testimony of women about new moon is not acceptable as well as in divorce. I asked him (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, if testimony of women is acceptable. He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, it is acceptable about virginity and childbirth.’”
